title: austronea ciliolata
intro: Austronea ciliolata, also known as the toothache plant, is a tropical plant species native to the Caribbean and Central America, characterized by its distinctive leaves with tooth-like edges. It has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it a potential remedy for toothache, fever, and other pain-related conditions. The health benefits of Austronea ciliolata include relief from pain, reduced inflammation, and improved respiratory function, while its medicinal properties also exhibit antimicrobial and antiseptic effects. Austronea ciliolata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can be crushed and applied topically to alleviate pain and inflammation.
